1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require "digest/sha1"
4: 
5: class Upload < ActiveRecord::Base
6:   include ActionView::Helpers::NumberHelper
7:   include HasUrl
8: 
9:   SHA1_LENGTH = 40
10:   SEEDED_ID_THRESHOLD = 0
11:   URL_REGEX = %r{(/original/\dX[/\.\w]*/(\h+)[\.\w]*)}
12:   MAX_IDENTIFY_SECONDS = 5
13:   DOMINANT_COLOR_COMMAND_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 5
14:   # the maximum length of a base62 encoded sha1
15:   MAX_BASE62_SHA1_LENGTH = 27
16: 
17:   belongs_to :user

184:   def fix_image_extension
185:     return false if extension == "unknown"
186: 
187:     begin
188:       # this is relatively cheap once cached
189:       original_path = Discourse.store.path_for(self)
190:       original_path = Discourse.store.download(self) if original_path.blank?
191: 
192:       image_info =
193:         begin
194:           image = FastImage.new(original_path)
195:           image.type # eager load to rescue errors early
196:           image
197:         rescue StandardError
198:           nil
199:         end
200:       new_extension = image_info&.type&.to_s || "unknown"
201: 
202:       if new_extension != self.extension
203:         self.update_columns(extension: new_extension)
204:         true
205:       end
206:     rescue StandardError
207:       self.update_columns(extension: "unknown")
208:       true
209:     end
210:   end

295:   def fix_dimensions!
296:     return if !FileHelper.is_supported_image?("image.#{extension}")
297: 
298:     begin
299:       path =
300:         if local?
301:           Discourse.store.path_for(self)
302:         else
303:           Discourse.store.download!(self)
304:         end
305: 
306:       if extension == "svg"
307:         w, h =
308:           begin
309:             Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
310:               "identify",
311:               "-ping",
312:               "-format",
313:               "%w %h",
314:               path,
315:               timeout: MAX_IDENTIFY_SECONDS,
316:             ).split(" ")
317:           rescue StandardError
318:             [0, 0]
319:           end
320:       else
321:         w, h = FastImage.new(path, raise_on_failure: true).size
322:       end
323: 
324:       self.width = w || 0
325:       self.height = h || 0
326: 
327:       self.thumbnail_width, self.thumbnail_height = ImageSizer.resize(w, h)
328: 
329:       self.update_columns(
330:         width: width,
331:         height: height,
332:         thumbnail_width: thumbnail_width,
333:         thumbnail_height: thumbnail_height,
334:       )
335:     rescue => e
336:       Discourse.warn_exception(e, message: "Error getting image dimensions")
337:     end
338:     nil
339:   end
340: 
341:   # on demand image size calculation, this allows us to null out image sizes
342:   # and still handle as needed
343:   def get_dimension(key)
344:     if v = self[key]
345:       return v
346:     end
347:     fix_dimensions!
348:     self[key]
349:   end
350:

352:     get_dimension(:width)
353:   end

357:   end

359:   def thumbnail_width
360:     get_dimension(:thumbnail_width)
361:   end
362: 
363:   def thumbnail_height
364:     get_dimension(:thumbnail_height)
365:   end

367:   def dominant_color(calculate_if_missing: false)
368:     val = read_attribute(:dominant_color)
369:     if val.nil? && calculate_if_missing
370:       calculate_dominant_color!
371:       read_attribute(:dominant_color)
372:     else
373:       val
374:     end
375:   end
376: 
377:   def calculate_dominant_color!(local_path = nil)
378:     color = nil
379: 
380:     color = "" if !FileHelper.is_supported_image?("image.#{extension}") || extension == "svg"
381: 
382:     if color.nil?
383:       local_path ||=
384:         if local?
385:           Discourse.store.path_for(self)
386:         else
387:           Discourse.store.download(self)
388:         end
389: 
390:       if local_path.nil?
391:         # Download failed. Could be too large to download, or file could be missing in s3
392:         color = ""
393:       end
394: 
395:       color ||=
396:         begin
397:           data =
398:             Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
399:               "nice",
400:               "-n",
401:               "10",
402:               "convert",
403:               local_path,
404:               "-depth",
405:               "8",
406:               "-resize",
407:               "1x1",
408:               "-define",
409:               "histogram:unique-colors=true",
410:               "-format",
411:               "%c",
412:               "histogram:info:",
413:               timeout: DOMINANT_COLOR_COMMAND_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
414:             )
415: 
416:           # Output format:
417:           # 1: (110.873,116.226,93.8821) #6F745E srgb(43.4798%,45.5789%,36.8165%)
418: 
419:           color = data[/#([0-9A-F]{6})/, 1]
420: 
421:           raise "Calculated dominant color but unable to parse output:\n#{data}" if color.nil?
422: 
423:           color
424:         rescue Discourse::Utils::CommandError
425:           # Timeout or unable to parse image
426:           # This can happen due to bad user input - ignore and save
427:           # an empty string to prevent re-evaluation
428:           ""
429:         end
430:     end
431: 
432:     if persisted?
433:       self.update_column(:dominant_color, color)
434:     else
435:       self.dominant_color = color
436:     end
437:   end
438: 
439:   def target_image_quality(local_path, test_quality)
440:     @file_quality ||=
441:       begin
442:         Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
443:           "identify",
444:           "-ping",
445:           "-format",
446:           "%Q",
447:           local_path,
448:           timeout: MAX_IDENTIFY_SECONDS,
449:         ).to_i
450:       rescue StandardError
451:         0
452:       end
453: 
454:     test_quality if @file_quality == 0 || @file_quality > test_quality
455:   end

469:   def self.sha1_from_base62_encoded(encoded_sha1)
470:     return nil if encoded_sha1.length > MAX_BASE62_SHA1_LENGTH
471:     sha1 = Base62.decode(encoded_sha1).to_s(16)
472: 
473:     if sha1.length > SHA1_LENGTH
474:       nil
475:     else
476:       sha1.rjust(SHA1_LENGTH, "0")
477:     end
478:   end

492:   def update_secure_status(source: "unknown", override: nil)
493:     if override.nil?
494:       mark_secure, reason = UploadSecurity.new(self).should_be_secure_with_reason
495:     else
496:       mark_secure = override
497:       reason = "manually overridden"
498:     end
499: 
500:     secure_status_did_change = self.secure? != mark_secure
501:     self.update(secure_params(mark_secure, reason, source))
502: 
503:     if secure_status_did_change && Discourse.store.external?
504:       Discourse.store.update_upload_access_control(self)
505:       sync_optimized_videos_secure_status(mark_secure)
506:     end
507: 
508:     secure_status_did_change
509:   end
510: 
511:   def sync_optimized_videos_secure_status(mark_secure)
512:     optimized_videos
513:       .includes(:optimized_upload)
514:       .each do |optimized_video|
515:         optimized_upload = optimized_video.optimized_upload
516:         if optimized_upload.secure? != mark_secure
517:           optimized_upload.update_secure_status(
518:             source: "sync_with_original_upload",
519:             override: mark_secure,
520:           )
521:         end
522:       end
523:   end
524: 
525:   def secure_params(secure, reason, source = "unknown")
526:     {
527:       secure: secure,
528:       security_last_changed_reason: reason + " | source: #{source}",
529:       security_last_changed_at: Time.zone.now,
530:     }
531:   end

533:   def self.migrate_to_new_scheme(limit: nil)
534:     problems = []
535: 
536:     DistributedMutex.synchronize("migrate_upload_to_new_scheme") do
537:       if SiteSetting.migrate_to_new_scheme
538:         max_file_size_kb = [
539:           SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb,
540:           SiteSetting.max_attachment_size_kb,
541:         ].max.kilobytes
542: 
543:         local_store = FileStore::LocalStore.new
544:         db = RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.current_db
545: 
546:         scope =
547:           Upload
548:             .by_users
549:             .where("url NOT LIKE '%/original/_X/%' AND url LIKE ?", "%/uploads/#{db}%")
550:             .order(id: :desc)
551: 
552:         scope = scope.limit(limit) if limit
553: 
554:         if scope.count == 0
555:           SiteSetting.migrate_to_new_scheme = false
556:           return problems
557:         end
558: 
559:         remap_scope = nil
560: 
561:         scope.each do |upload|
562:           begin
563:             # keep track of the url
564:             previous_url = upload.url.dup
565:             # where is the file currently stored?
566:             external = previous_url =~ %r{\A//}
567:             # download if external
568:             if external
569:               url = SiteSetting.scheme + ":" + previous_url
570: 
571:               begin
572:                 retries ||= 0
573: 
574:                 file =
575:                   FileHelper.download(
576:                     url,
577:                     max_file_size: max_file_size_kb,
578:                     tmp_file_name: "discourse",
579:                     follow_redirect: true,
580:                   )
581:               rescue OpenURI::HTTPError
582:                 retry if (retries += 1) < 1
583:                 next
584:               end
585: 
586:               path = file.path
587:             else
588:               path = local_store.path_for(upload)
589:             end
590:             # compute SHA if missing
591:             upload.sha1 = Upload.generate_digest(path) if upload.sha1.blank?
592: 
593:             # store to new location & update the filesize
594:             File.open(path) do |f|
595:               upload.url = Discourse.store.store_upload(f, upload)
596:               upload.filesize = f.size
597:               upload.save!(validate: false)
598:             end
599:             # remap the URLs
600:             DbHelper.remap(UrlHelper.absolute(previous_url), upload.url) unless external
601: 
602:             DbHelper.remap(
603:               previous_url,
604:               upload.url,
605:               excluded_tables: %w[
606:                 posts
607:                 post_search_data
608:                 incoming_emails
609:                 notifications
610:                 single_sign_on_records
611:                 stylesheet_cache
612:                 topic_search_data
613:                 users
614:                 user_emails
615:                 draft_sequences
616:                 optimized_images
617:               ],
618:             )
619: 
620:             remap_scope ||=
621:               begin
622:                 Post
623:                   .with_deleted
624:                   .where(
625:                     "raw ~ '/uploads/#{db}/\\d+/' OR raw ~ '/uploads/#{db}/original/(\\d|[a-z])/'",
626:                   )
627:                   .select(:id, :raw, :cooked)
628:                   .all
629:               end
630: 
631:             remap_scope.each do |post|
632:               post.raw.gsub!(previous_url, upload.url)
633:               post.cooked.gsub!(previous_url, upload.url)
634:               if post.changed?
635:                 Post.with_deleted.where(id: post.id).update_all(raw: post.raw, cooked: post.cooked)
636:               end
637:             end
638: 
639:             upload.optimized_images.find_each(&:destroy!)
640:             upload.rebake_posts_on_old_scheme
641:             # remove the old file (when local)
642:             FileUtils.rm(path, force: true) unless external
643:           rescue => e
644:             problems << { upload: upload, ex: e }
645:           ensure
646:             file&.unlink
647:             file&.close
648:           end
649:         end
650:       end
651:     end
652: 
653:     problems
654:   end
655: 
656:   def self.extract_upload_ids(raw)
657:     return [] if raw.blank?
658: 
659:     sha1s = []
660: 
661:     raw.scan(/\/(\h{40})/).each { |match| sha1s << match[0] }
662: 
663:     raw
664:       .scan(%r{/([a-zA-Z0-9]+)})
665:       .each { |match| sha1s << Upload.sha1_from_base62_encoded(match[0]) }
666: 
667:     Upload.where(sha1: sha1s.uniq).pluck(:id)
668:   end
669: 
670:   def self.backfill_dominant_colors!(count)
671:     Upload
672:       .where(dominant_color: nil)
673:       .order("id desc")
674:       .first(count)
675:       .each { |upload| upload.calculate_dominant_color! }
676:   end

686:       SiteSetting.Upload.enable_s3_uploads && FileHelper.is_supported_video?(original_filename) &&
687:       !OptimizedVideo.exists?(upload_id: id)
688:   end

691:     Jobs.enqueue(:convert_video, upload_id: id)
692:   end

695: # == Schema Information
696: #
697: # Table name: uploads
698: #
699: #  id                           :integer          not null, primary key
700: #  user_id                      :integer          not null
701: #  original_filename            :string           not null
702: #  filesize                     :bigint           not null
703: #  width                        :integer
704: #  height                       :integer
705: #  url                          :string           not null
706: #  created_at                   :datetime         not null
707: #  updated_at                   :datetime         not null
708: #  sha1                         :string(40)
709: #  origin                       :string(2000)
710: #  retain_hours                 :integer
711: #  extension                    :string(10)

3: class UploadReference < ActiveRecord::Base
4:   belongs_to :upload
5:   belongs_to :target, polymorphic: true
6: 
7:   delegate :to_markdown, to: :upload
8: 
9:   def self.ensure_exist!(upload_ids: [], target: nil, target_type: nil, target_id: nil)
10:     if !target && !(target_type && target_id)
11:       raise "target OR target_type and target_id are required"
12:     end
13: 
14:     if target.present?
15:       target_type = target.class
16:       target_id = target.id
17:     end
18: 
19:     upload_ids = upload_ids.uniq.reject(&:blank?)
20:     target_type = target_type.to_s
21: 
22:     if upload_ids.empty?
23:       UploadReference.where(target_type: target_type, target_id: target_id).delete_all
24: 
25:       return
26:     end
27: 
28:     rows =
29:       upload_ids.map do |upload_id|
30:         {
31:           upload_id: upload_id,
32:           target_type: target_type,
33:           target_id: target_id,
34:           created_at: Time.zone.now,
35:           updated_at: Time.zone.now,
36:         }
37:       end
38: 
39:     UploadReference.transaction do |transaction|
40:       UploadReference
41:         .where(target_type: target_type, target_id: target_id)
42:         .where.not(upload_id: upload_ids)
43:         .delete_all
44: 
45:       UploadReference.insert_all(rows)
46:     end
47:   end
48: end
49:

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: ##
4: # A note on determining whether an upload should be marked as secure:
5: #
6: # Some of these flags checked (e.g. all of the for_X flags and the opts[:type])
7: # are only set when _initially uploading_ via UploadCreator and are not present
8: # when an upload already exists, these will only be checked when the @creating
9: # option is present.
10: #
11: # If the upload already exists the best way to figure out whether it should be
12: # secure alongside the site settings is the access_control_post_id, because the
13: # original post the upload is linked to has far more bearing on its security context
14: # post-upload. If the access_control_post_id does not exist then we just rely
15: # on the current secure? status, otherwise there would be a lot of additional
16: # complex queries and joins to perform.
17: #
18: # These queries will be performed only if the @creating option is false. So if
19: # an upload is included in a post, and it's an upload from a different source
20: # (e.g. a category logo, site setting upload) then we will determine secure
21: # state _based on the first place the upload was referenced_.
22: #
23: # NOTE: When updating this to add more cases where uploads will be marked
24: # secure, consider uploads:secure_upload_analyse_and_update as well, which
25: # does not use this class directly but uses an SQL version of its rules for
26: # efficient updating of many uploads in bulk.
27: class UploadSecurity
28:   @@custom_public_types = []
29: 
30:   PUBLIC_TYPES = %w[
31:     avatar
32:     custom_emoji
33:     profile_background
34:     card_background
35:     category_logo
36:     category_logo_dark
37:     category_background
38:     group_flair
39:     badge_image
40:     site_setting
41:   ]
42: 
43:   PUBLIC_UPLOAD_REFERENCE_TYPES = %w[
44:     Badge
45:     Category
46:     CustomEmoji
47:     Group
48:     SiteSetting
49:     ThemeField
50:     User
51:     UserAvatar
52:     UserProfile
53:   ]
54: 
55:   def self.register_custom_public_type(type)
56:     @@custom_public_types << type if !@@custom_public_types.include?(type)
57:   end
58: 
59:   # used in tests
60:   def self.reset_custom_public_types
61:     @@custom_public_types = []
62:   end
63: 
64:   def initialize(upload, opts = {})
65:     @upload = upload
66:     @opts = opts
67:     @upload_type = @opts[:type]
68:     @creating = @opts[:creating]
69:   end
70: 
71:   def should_be_secure?
72:     should_be_secure_with_reason.first
73:   end
74: 
75:   def should_be_secure_with_reason
76:     insecure_context_checks.each { |check, reason| return false, reason if perform_check(check) }
77:     secure_context_checks.each do |check, reason|
78:       return perform_check(check), reason if priority_check?(check)
79:       return true, reason if perform_check(check)
80:     end
81: 
82:     [false, "no checks satisfied"]
83:   end
84: 
85:   private
86: 
87:   def access_control_post
88:     @access_control_post ||=
89:       @upload.access_control_post_id.present? ? @upload.access_control_post : nil
90:   end
91: 
92:   def insecure_context_checks
93:     {
94:       secure_uploads_disabled: "secure uploads is disabled",
95:       insecure_creation_for_modifiers: "one or more creation for_modifiers was satisfied",
96:       public_type: "upload is public type",
97:       regular_emoji: "upload is used for regular emoji",
98:       publicly_referenced_first: "upload was publicly referenced when it was first created",
99:     }
100:   end
101: 
102:   def secure_context_checks
103:     {
104:       login_required: "login is required",
105:       access_control_post_should_secure_uploads: "access control post dictates security",
106:       secure_creation_for_modifiers: "one or more creation for_modifiers was satisfied",
107:       uploading_in_composer: "uploading via the composer",
108:       already_secure: "upload is already secure",
109:     }
110:   end
111: 
112:   # The access control check is important because that is the truest indicator
113:   # of whether an upload should be secure or not, and thus should be returned
114:   # immediately if there is an access control post.
115:   def priority_check?(check)
116:     check == :access_control_post_should_secure_uploads && access_control_post
117:   end
118: 
119:   def perform_check(check)
120:     send("#{check}_check")
121:   end
122: 
123:   #### START PUBLIC CHECKS ####
124: 
125:   def secure_uploads_disabled_check
126:     !SiteSetting.secure_uploads?
127:   end
128: 
129:   def insecure_creation_for_modifiers_check
130:     return false if !@creating
131:     @upload.for_theme || @upload.for_site_setting || @upload.for_gravatar
132:   end
133: 
134:   def public_type_check
135:     PUBLIC_TYPES.include?(@upload_type) || @@custom_public_types.include?(@upload_type)
136:   end
137: 
138:   def publicly_referenced_first_check
139:     return false if @creating
140:     first_reference =
141:       @upload
142:         .upload_references
143:         .joins(<<~SQL)
144:           LEFT JOIN posts ON upload_references.target_type = 'Post' AND upload_references.target_id = posts.id
145:         SQL
146:         .where("posts.deleted_at IS NULL")
147:         .order("upload_references.created_at ASC, upload_references.id ASC")
148:         .first
149:     return false if first_reference.blank?
150:     PUBLIC_UPLOAD_REFERENCE_TYPES.include?(first_reference.target_type)
151:   end
152: 
153:   def regular_emoji_check
154:     return false if @upload.origin.blank?
155:     uri = URI.parse(@upload.origin)
156:     return true if Emoji.all.map(&:url).include?("#{uri.path}?#{uri.query}")
157:     uri.path.include?("images/emoji")
158:   end
159: 
160:   #### END PUBLIC CHECKS ####
161: 
162:   #--------------------------#
163: 
164:   #### START PRIVATE CHECKS ####
165: 
166:   def login_required_check
167:     SiteSetting.login_required? && !SiteSetting.secure_uploads_pm_only?
168:   end
169: 
170:   # Whether the upload should remain secure or not after posting depends on its context,
171:   # which is based on the post it is linked to via access_control_post_id.
172:   #
173:   # If that post should_secure_uploads? then the upload should also be secure.
174:   #
175:   # This may change to false if the upload was set to secure on upload e.g. in
176:   # a post composer then it turned out that the post itself was not in a secure context.
177:   #
178:   # A post is with secure uploads if it is a private message or in a read restricted
179:   # category. See `Post#should_secure_uploads?` for the full definition.
180:   def access_control_post_should_secure_uploads_check
181:     access_control_post&.should_secure_uploads?
182:   end
183: 
184:   def uploading_in_composer_check
185:     @upload_type == "composer"
186:   end
187: 
188:   def secure_creation_for_modifiers_check
189:     return false if !@creating
190:     @upload.for_private_message || @upload.for_group_message
191:   end
192: 
193:   def already_secure_check
194:     @upload.secure?
195:   end
196: 
197:   #### END PRIVATE CHECKS ####
198: end

(End of file - total 198 lines)
</content>

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class OptimizedImage < ActiveRecord::Base
4:   include HasUrl
5:   belongs_to :upload
6: 
7:   # BUMP UP if optimized image algorithm changes
8:   VERSION = 2
9:   URL_REGEX = %r{(/optimized/\dX[/\.\w]*/([a-zA-Z0-9]+)[\.\w]*)}
10: 
11:   def self.lock(upload_id, width, height)
12:     @hostname ||= Discourse.os_hostname
13:     # note, the extra lock here ensures we only optimize one image per machine on webs
14:     # this can very easily lead to runaway CPU so slowing it down is beneficial and it is hijacked
15:     #
16:     # we can not afford this blocking in Sidekiq cause it can lead to starvation
17:     if lock_per_machine?
18:       DistributedMutex.synchronize("optimized_image_host_#{@hostname}") do
19:         DistributedMutex.synchronize("optimized_image_#{upload_id}_#{width}_#{height}") { yield }
20:       end
21:     else
22:       DistributedMutex.synchronize("optimized_image_#{upload_id}_#{width}_#{height}") { yield }
23:     end
24:   end
25: 
26:   def self.lock_per_machine?
27:     return @lock_per_machine if defined?(@lock_per_machine)
28:     @lock_per_machine = !Sidekiq.server?
29:   end
30: 
31:   def self.lock_per_machine=(value)
32:     @lock_per_machine = value
33:   end
34: 
35:   def self.create_for(upload, width, height, opts = {})
36:     return if width <= 0 || height <= 0
37:     return if upload.try(:sha1).blank?
38: 
39:     # no extension so try to guess it
40:     upload.fix_image_extension if (!upload.extension)
41: 
42:     if !upload.extension.match?(IM_DECODERS)
43:       if opts[:raise_on_error]
44:         raise InvalidAccess
45:       else
46:         # nothing to do ... bad extension, not an image
47:         return
48:       end
49:     end
50: 
51:     # prefer to look up the thumbnail without grabbing any locks
52:     extension = ".#{opts[:format] || upload.extension}"
53:     thumbnail = find_by(upload_id: upload.id, width: width, height: height, extension: extension)
54: 
55:     # correct bad thumbnail if needed
56:     if thumbnail && (thumbnail.url.blank? || thumbnail.version != VERSION)
57:       thumbnail.destroy!
58:       thumbnail = nil
59:     end
60: 
61:     return thumbnail if thumbnail
62: 
63:     store = Discourse.store
64: 
65:     # create the thumbnail otherwise
66:     original_path = store.path_for(upload)
67: 
68:     if original_path.blank?
69:       # download is protected with a DistributedMutex
70:       original_path = store.download(upload)
71:     end
72: 
73:     if extension == ".svg" && upload.extension != "svg"
74:       if opts[:raise_on_error]
75:         raise InvalidAccess
76:       else
77:         # we can not convert any images to svg, unsupported
78:         return
79:       end
80:     end
81: 
82:     lock(upload.id, width, height) do
83:       # may have been generated since we got the lock
84:       thumbnail = find_by(upload_id: upload.id, width: width, height: height, extension: extension)
85: 
86:       # return the previous thumbnail if any
87:       return thumbnail if thumbnail
88: 
89:       if original_path.blank?
90:         Rails.logger.error("Could not find file in the store located at url: #{upload.url}")
91:       else
92:         # create a temp file with the same extension as the original
93: 
94:         return nil if extension.length == 1
95: 
96:         temp_file = Tempfile.new(["discourse-thumbnail", extension])
97:         temp_path = temp_file.path
98: 
99:         target_quality =
100:           upload.target_image_quality(
101:             original_path,
102:             SiteSetting.ImageQuality.image_preview_jpg_quality,
103:           )
104:         opts = opts.merge(quality: target_quality) if target_quality
105:         opts = opts.merge(upload_id: upload.id)
106: 
107:         # special case, when "resizing" vectors we simply copy
108:         if extension == ".svg"
109:           FileUtils.cp(original_path, temp_path)
110:           resized = true
111:         elsif opts[:crop]
112:           resized = crop(original_path, temp_path, width, height, opts)
113:         else
114:           resized = resize(original_path, temp_path, width, height, opts)
115:         end
116: 
117:         if resized
118:           # TODO: crop vs resize should be stored in the db, quality should be stored
119:           thumbnail =
120:             OptimizedImage.create!(
121:               upload_id: upload.id,
122:               sha1: Upload.generate_digest(temp_path),
123:               extension: extension,
124:               width: width,
125:               height: height,
126:               url: "",
127:               filesize: File.size(temp_path),
128:               version: VERSION,
129:             )
130: 
131:           # store the optimized image and update its url
132:           File.open(temp_path) do |file|
133:             url = store.store_optimized_image(file, thumbnail, nil, secure: upload.secure?)
134:             if url.present?
135:               thumbnail.url = url
136:               thumbnail.save
137:             else
138:               Rails.logger.error(

186:   def self.safe_path?(path)
187:     # this matches instructions which call #to_s

194:   def self.ensure_safe_paths!(*paths)

196:   end

198:   IM_DECODERS = /\A(jpe?g|png|ico|gif|webp|avif|svg)\z/i
199: 
200:   def self.prepend_decoder!(path, ext_path = nil, opts = nil)
201:     opts ||= {}
202: 
203:     # This logic is a little messy but the result of using mocks for most
204:     # of the image tests. The idea here is you shouldn't trust the "original"
205:     # path of a file to figure out its extension. However, in certain cases
206:     # such as generating the loading upload thumbnail, we force the format,
207:     # and this allows us to use the forced format in that case.
208:     extension = nil
209:     if (opts[:format] && path != ext_path)
210:       extension = File.extname(path)[1..-1]
211:     else
212:       extension = File.extname(opts[:filename] || ext_path || path)[1..-1]
213:     end
214: 
215:     if !extension || !extension.match?(IM_DECODERS)
216:       raise Discourse::InvalidAccess.new("Unsupported extension: #{extension}")
217:     end
218:     "#{extension}:#{path}"
219:   end

225:   def self.resize_instructions(from, to, dimensions, opts = {})
226:     ensure_safe_paths!(from, to)
227: 
228:     # note FROM my not be named correctly
229:     from = prepend_decoder!(from, to, opts)
230:     to = prepend_decoder!(to, to, opts)
231: 
232:     instructions = ["convert", "#{from}[0]"]
233: 
234:     instructions << "-colors" << opts[:colors].to_s if opts[:colors]
235: 
236:     instructions << "-quality" << opts[:quality].to_s if opts[:quality]
237: 
238:     # NOTE: ORDER is important!
239:     instructions.concat(
240:       %W[
241:         -auto-orient
242:         -gravity
243:         center
244:         -background
245:         transparent
246:         -#{thumbnail_or_resize}
247:         #{dimensions}^
248:         -extent
249:         #{dimensions}
250:         -interpolate
251:         catrom
252:         -unsharp
253:         2x0.5+0.7+0
254:         -interlace
255:         none
256:         -profile

258:         #{to}

272:       -auto-orient
273:       -gravity
274:       north
275:       -background
276:       transparent
277:       -#{thumbnail_or_resize}
278:       #{dimensions}^
279:       -crop

283:       -interlace

285:       -profile
286:       #{File.join(Rails.root, "vendor", "data", "RT_sRGB.icm")}

299: 
300:     %W{

304:       -gravity

306:       -background

319:     optimize("resize", from, to, "#{width}x#{height}", opts)

330:   def self.optimize(operation, from, to, dimensions, opts = {})
331:     method_name = "#{operation}_instructions"
332: 
333:     instructions = self.public_send(method_name.to_sym, from, to, dimensions, opts)
334:     convert_with(instructions, to, opts)
335:   end

337:   MAX_PNGQUANT_SIZE = 500_000
338:   MAX_CONVERT_SECONDS = 20

340:   def self.convert_with(instructions, to, opts = {})
341:     Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
342:       "nice",
343:       "-n",
344:       "10",
345:       *instructions,
346:       timeout: MAX_CONVERT_SECONDS,
347:     )
348: 
349:     allow_pngquant = to.downcase.ends_with?(".png") && File.size(to) < MAX_PNGQUANT_SIZE
350:     FileHelper.optimize_image!(to, allow_pngquant: allow_pngquant)
351:     true
352:   rescue => e
353:     if opts[:raise_on_error]
354:       raise e
355:     else
356:       error = +"Failed to optimize image:"
357: 
358:       if e.message =~ /\Aconvert:([^`]+)/
359:         error << $1
360:       else
361:         error << " unknown reason"
362:       end
363: 
364:       Discourse.warn(
365:         error,
366:         upload_id: opts[:upload_id],
367:         location: to,
368:         error_message: e.message,
369:         instructions: instructions,
370:       )
371:       false
372:     end
373:   end

3: module HasUrl

6:   class_methods do
7:     def extract_url(url)
8:       url.match(self::URL_REGEX)
9:     end

11:     def extract_sha1(path)
12:       data = extract_url(path)
13:       return if data.blank?
14: 
15:       sha1 = data[2]
16:       return if sha1&.length != Upload::SHA1_LENGTH
17: 
18:       sha1

21:     def get_from_url(url)
22:       return if url.blank?
23: 
24:       uri =
25:         begin
26:           URI(UrlHelper.unencode(url))
27:         rescue URI::Error
28:         end
29: 
30:       return if uri&.path.blank?
31:       data = extract_url(uri.path)
32: 
33:       if data.blank?
34:         result = nil
35:         result ||= self.find_by(url: uri.path)
36:         return result
37:       end
38: 
39:       result = nil
40: 
41:       if self.name == "Upload"
42:         sha1 = data[2]
43:         result = self.find_by(sha1: sha1) if sha1&.length == Upload::SHA1_LENGTH
44:       end
45: 
46:       result || self.find_by("url LIKE ?", "%#{data[1]}")
47:     end
48: 
49:     def get_from_urls(upload_urls)
50:       urls = []
51:       sha1s = []
52: 
53:       upload_urls.each do |url|
54:         next if url.blank?
55: 
56:         uri =
57:           begin
58:             URI(UrlHelper.unencode(url))
59:           rescue URI::Error
60:           end
61: 
62:         next if uri&.path.blank?
63:         urls << uri.path
64: 
65:         if data = extract_url(uri.path).presence
66:           urls << data[1]
67:           sha1s << data[2] if self.name == "Upload"
68:         end
69:       end
70: 
71:       self.where(url: urls).or(self.where(sha1: sha1s))
72:     end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require "fastimage"
4: 
5: class UploadCreator
6:   TYPES_TO_CROP = %w[avatar card_background custom_emoji profile_background].each(&:freeze)
7: 
8:   ALLOWED_SVG_ELEMENTS = %w[
9:     circle
10:     clipPath
11:     defs
12:     ellipse
13:     feGaussianBlur
14:     filter
15:     g
16:     line
17:     linearGradient
18:     marker
19:     path
20:     polygon
21:     polyline
22:     radialGradient
23:     rect
24:     stop
25:     style
26:     svg
27:     text
28:     textPath
29:     tref
30:     tspan
31:     use
32:   ].each(&:freeze)
33: 
34:   # Available options
35:   #  - type (string)
36:   #  - origin (string)
37:   #  - for_group_message (boolean)
38:   #  - for_theme (boolean)
39:   #  - for_private_message (boolean)
40:   #  - pasted (boolean)
41:   #  - for_export (boolean)
42:   #  - for_gravatar (boolean)
43:   #  - skip_validations (boolean)
44:   def initialize(file, filename, opts = {})
45:     @file = file
46:     @filename = (filename || "").gsub(/[^[:print:]]/, "")
47:     @upload = Upload.new(original_filename: @filename, filesize: 0)
48:     @opts = opts
49:     @filesize = @opts[:filesize] if @opts[:external_upload_too_big]
50:     @opts[:validate] = (
51:       if opts[:skip_validations].present?
52:         !ActiveRecord::Type::Boolean.new.cast(opts[:skip_validations])
53:       else
54:         true
55:       end
56:     )
57:   end
58: 
59:   def create_for(user_id)
60:     if filesize <= 0
61:       @upload.errors.add(:base, I18n.t("upload.empty"))
62:       return @upload
63:     end
64: 
65:     @image_info =
66:       begin
67:         image = FastImage.new(@file)
68:         image.type # eager load to rescue errors early
69:         image
70:       rescue StandardError
71:         nil
72:       end
73:     is_image = FileHelper.is_supported_image?(@filename)
74:     is_image ||= @image_info && FileHelper.is_supported_image?("test.#{@image_info.type}")
75:     is_image = false if @opts[:for_theme]
76:     is_thumbnail = SiteSetting.video_thumbnails_enabled && @opts[:type] == "thumbnail"
77: 
78:     # If this is present then it means we are creating an upload record from
79:     # an external_upload_stub and the file is > ExternalUploadManager::DOWNLOAD_LIMIT,
80:     # so we have not downloaded it to a tempfile. no modifications can be made to the
81:     # file in this case because it does not exist; we simply move it to its new location
82:     # in S3
83:     #
84:     # FIXME: I've added a bunch of external_upload_too_big checks littered
85:     # throughout the UploadCreator code. It would be better to have two separate
86:     # classes with shared methods, rather than doing all these checks all over the
87:     # place. Needs a refactor.
88:     external_upload_too_big = @opts[:external_upload_too_big]
89:     sha1_before_changes = Upload.generate_digest(@file) if @file
90: 
91:     DistributedMutex.synchronize("upload_#{user_id}_#{@filename}") do
92:       # We need to convert HEIFs early because FastImage does not consider them as images
93:       if convert_heif_to_jpeg? && !external_upload_too_big
94:         convert_heif!
95:         is_image = FileHelper.is_supported_image?("test.#{@image_info.type}")
96:       end
97: 
98:       if is_image && !external_upload_too_big
99:         extract_image_info!
100:         return @upload if @upload.errors.present?
101: 
102:         if @image_info.type == :svg
103:           clean_svg!
104:         elsif @image_info.type == :ico
105:           convert_favicon_to_png!
106:         elsif !Rails.env.test? || @opts[:force_optimize]
107:           convert_to_jpeg! if convert_png_to_jpeg? || should_alter_quality?
108:           fix_orientation! if should_fix_orientation?
109:           crop! if should_crop?
110:           optimize! if should_optimize?
111:           downsize! if should_downsize?
112:           return @upload if is_still_too_big?
113:         end
114: 
115:         # conversion may have switched the type
116:         image_type = @image_info.type.to_s
117:       end
118: 
119:       # compute the sha of the file and generate a unique hash
120:       # which is only used for secure uploads
121:       sha1 = Upload.generate_digest(@file) if !external_upload_too_big
122:       unique_hash = generate_fake_sha1_hash if SiteSetting.secure_uploads ||
123:         external_upload_too_big || is_thumbnail
124: 
125:       # we do not check for duplicate uploads if secure uploads is
126:       # enabled because we use a unique access hash to differentiate
127:       # between uploads instead of the sha1, and to get around various
128:       # access/permission issues for uploads
129:       # We do not check for duplicate uploads for video thumbnails because
130:       # their filename needs to match with their corresponding video. This also
131:       # enables rebuilding the html on a topic to regenerate a thumbnail.
132:       if !SiteSetting.secure_uploads && !external_upload_too_big && !is_thumbnail
133:         # do we already have that upload?
134:         @upload = Upload.find_by(sha1: sha1)
135: 
136:         # make sure the previous upload has not failed
137:         if @upload && @upload.url.blank?
138:           @upload.destroy
139:           @upload = nil
140:         end
141: 
142:         # return the previous upload if any
143:         if @upload
144:           add_metadata!
145:           UserUpload.find_or_create_by!(user_id: user_id, upload_id: @upload.id) if user_id
146:           return @upload
147:         end
148:       end
149: 
150:       fixed_original_filename = nil
151: 
152:       if is_image && !external_upload_too_big
153:         current_extension = File.extname(@filename).downcase.sub("jpeg", "jpg")
154:         expected_extension = ".#{image_type}".downcase.sub("jpeg", "jpg")
155: 
156:         # we have to correct original filename here, no choice
157:         # otherwise validation will fail and we can not save
158:         # TODO decide if we only run the validation on the extension
159:         if current_extension != expected_extension
160:           basename = File.basename(@filename, current_extension).presence || "image"
161:           fixed_original_filename = "#{basename}#{expected_extension}"
162:         end
163:       end
164: 
165:       # create the upload otherwise
166:       @upload = Upload.new
167:       @upload.user_id = user_id
168:       @upload.original_filename = fixed_original_filename || @filename
169:       @upload.filesize = filesize
170:       @upload.sha1 =
171:         (
172:           if (SiteSetting.secure_uploads? || external_upload_too_big || is_thumbnail)
173:             unique_hash
174:           else
175:             sha1
176:           end
177:         )
178:       @upload.original_sha1 = SiteSetting.secure_uploads? ? sha1 : nil
179:       @upload.url = ""
180:       @upload.origin = @opts[:origin][0...2000] if @opts[:origin]
181:       @upload.extension = image_type || File.extname(@filename)[1..10]
182: 
183:       if is_image && !external_upload_too_big
184:         if @image_info.type.to_s == "svg"
185:           w, h = [0, 0]
186: 
187:           # identify can behave differently depending on how it's compiled and
188:           # what programs (e.g. inkscape) are installed on your system.
189:           # 'MSVG:' forces ImageMagick to use internal routines and behave
190:           # consistently whether it's running from our docker container or not
191:           begin
192:             w, h =
193:               Discourse::Utils
194:                 .execute_command(
195:                   "identify",
196:                   "-ping",
197:                   "-format",
198:                   "%w %h",
199:                   "MSVG:#{@file.path}",
200:                   timeout: Upload::MAX_IDENTIFY_SECONDS,
201:                 )
202:                 .split(" ")
203:                 .map(&:to_i)
204:           rescue StandardError
205:             # use default 0, 0
206:           end
207:         else
208:           w, h = @image_info.size
209:         end
210: 
211:         @upload.thumbnail_width, @upload.thumbnail_height = ImageSizer.resize(w, h)
212:         @upload.width, @upload.height = w, h
213:         @upload.animated = animated?
214:         @upload.calculate_dominant_color!(@file.path)
215:       end
216: 
217:       add_metadata!
218: 
219:       if SiteSetting.secure_uploads
220:         secure, reason =
221:           UploadSecurity.new(@upload, @opts.merge(creating: true)).should_be_secure_with_reason
222:         attrs = @upload.secure_params(secure, reason, "upload creator")
223:         @upload.assign_attributes(attrs)
224:       end
225: 
226:       # Callbacks using this event to validate the upload or the file must add errors to the
227:       # upload errors object.
228:       #
229:       # Can't do any validation of the file if external_upload_too_big because we don't have
230:       # the actual file.
231:       if !external_upload_too_big
232:         DiscourseEvent.trigger(:before_upload_creation, @file, is_image, @upload, @opts[:validate])
233:       end
234:       return @upload unless @upload.errors.empty? && @upload.save(validate: @opts[:validate])
235: 
236:       should_move = false
237:       upload_changed =
238:         if external_upload_too_big
239:           false
240:         else
241:           Upload.generate_digest(@file) != sha1_before_changes
242:         end
243: 
244:       store = Discourse.store
245: 
246:       if @opts[:existing_external_upload_key] && store.external?
247:         should_move = external_upload_too_big || !upload_changed
248:       end
249: 
250:       if should_move
251:         # move the file in the store instead of reuploading
252:         url =
253:           store.move_existing_stored_upload(
254:             existing_external_upload_key: @opts[:existing_external_upload_key],
255:             upload: @upload,
256:           )
257:       else
258:         # store the file and update its url
259:         File.open(@file.path) { |f| url = store.store_upload(f, @upload) }
260:         if @opts[:existing_external_upload_key]
261:           store.delete_file(@opts[:existing_external_upload_key])
262:         end
263:       end
264: 
265:       if url.present?
266:         @upload.url = url
267:         @upload.save!(validate: @opts[:validate])
268:       else
269:         @upload.errors.add(
270:           :url,
271:           I18n.t("upload.store_failure", upload_id: @upload.id, user_id: user_id),
272:         )
273:       end
274: 
275:       if @upload.errors.empty? && is_image && @opts[:type] == "avatar" && @upload.extension != "svg"
276:         Jobs.enqueue(:create_avatar_thumbnails, upload_id: @upload.id)
277:       end
278: 
279:       if @upload.errors.empty?
280:         UserUpload.find_or_create_by!(user_id: user_id, upload_id: @upload.id) if user_id
281:       end
282: 
283:       @upload
284:     end
285:   ensure
286:     if @file
287:       @file.respond_to?(:close!) ? @file.close! : @file.close
288:     end
289:   end
290: 
291:   def extract_image_info!
292:     @image_info =
293:       begin
294:         image = FastImage.new(@file)
295:         image.type # eager load to rescue errors early
296:         image
297:       rescue StandardError
298:         nil
299:       end
300:     @file.rewind
301: 
302:     if @image_info.nil?
303:       @upload.errors.add(:base, I18n.t("upload.images.not_supported_or_corrupted"))
304:     elsif filesize <= 0
305:       @upload.errors.add(:base, I18n.t("upload.empty"))
306:     elsif pixels == 0 && @image_info.type.to_s != "svg"
307:       @upload.errors.add(:base, I18n.t("upload.images.size_not_found"))
308:     elsif max_image_pixels > 0 && pixels >= max_image_pixels
309:       @upload.errors.add(
310:         :base,
311:         I18n.t(
312:           "upload.images.larger_than_x_megapixels",
313:           max_image_megapixels: SiteSetting.max_image_megapixels,
314:           original_filename: @upload.original_filename,
315:         ),
316:       )
317:     end
318:   end
319: 
320:   MIN_PIXELS_TO_CONVERT_TO_JPEG = 1280 * 720
321: 
322:   def convert_png_to_jpeg?
323:     return false unless @image_info.type == :png
324:     return true if @opts[:pasted]
325:     return false if SiteSetting.ImageQuality.png_to_jpg_quality == 100
326:     pixels > MIN_PIXELS_TO_CONVERT_TO_JPEG
327:   end

330:   MIN_CONVERT_TO_JPEG_SAVING_RATIO = 0.70

332:   def convert_favicon_to_png!
333:     png_tempfile = Tempfile.new(%w[image .png])
334: 
335:     from = @file.path
336:     to = png_tempfile.path
337: 
338:     OptimizedImage.ensure_safe_paths!(from, to)
339: 
340:     from = OptimizedImage.prepend_decoder!(from, nil, filename: "image.#{@image_info.type}")
341:     to = OptimizedImage.prepend_decoder!(to)
342: 
343:     from = "#{from}[-1]" # We only want the last(largest) image of the .ico file
344: 
345:     opts = { flatten: false } # Preserve transparency
346: 
347:     begin
348:       execute_convert(from, to, opts)
349:     rescue StandardError
350:       # retry with debugging enabled
351:       execute_convert(from, to, opts.merge(debug: true))
352:     end
353: 
354:     @file.respond_to?(:close!) ? @file.close! : @file.close
355:     @file = png_tempfile

359:   def convert_to_jpeg!
360:     return if @opts[:for_site_setting]
361:     return if filesize < MIN_CONVERT_TO_JPEG_BYTES_SAVED
362: 
363:     jpeg_tempfile = Tempfile.new(%w[image .jpg])
364: 
365:     from = @file.path
366:     to = jpeg_tempfile.path
367: 
368:     OptimizedImage.ensure_safe_paths!(from, to)
369: 
370:     from = OptimizedImage.prepend_decoder!(from, nil, filename: "image.#{@image_info.type}")
371:     to = OptimizedImage.prepend_decoder!(to)
372: 
373:     opts = {}
374: 
375:     desired_quality = [
376:       SiteSetting.ImageQuality.png_to_jpg_quality,
377:       SiteSetting.ImageQuality.recompress_original_jpg_quality,
378:     ].compact.min
379: 
380:     target_quality = @upload.target_image_quality(from, desired_quality)
381:     opts = { quality: target_quality } if target_quality
382: 
383:     begin
384:       execute_convert(from, to, opts)
385:     rescue StandardError
386:       # retry with debugging enabled
387:       execute_convert(from, to, opts.merge(debug: true))
388:     end
389: 
390:     new_size = File.size(jpeg_tempfile.path)
391: 
392:     keep_jpeg = new_size < filesize * MIN_CONVERT_TO_JPEG_SAVING_RATIO
393:     keep_jpeg &&= (filesize - new_size) > MIN_CONVERT_TO_JPEG_BYTES_SAVED
394: 
395:     if keep_jpeg
396:       @file.respond_to?(:close!) ? @file.close! : @file.close
397:       @file = jpeg_tempfile
398:       extract_image_info!
399:     else
400:       jpeg_tempfile.close!
401:     end
402:   end

416:     rescue StandardError

427:   def execute_convert(from, to, opts = {})
428:     command = ["magick", from, "-auto-orient", "-background", "white", "-interlace", "none"]
429:     command << "-flatten" unless opts[:flatten] == false
430:     command << "-debug" << "all" if opts[:debug]
431:     command << "-quality" << opts[:quality].to_s if opts[:quality]
432:     command << to
433: 
434:     Discourse::Utils.execute_command(
435:       *command,
436:       failure_message: I18n.t("upload.png_to_jpg_conversion_failure_message"),
437:       timeout: MAX_CONVERT_FORMAT_SECONDS,
438:     )
439:   end

444:     desired_quality =

448:         else
449:           SiteSetting.ImageQuality.recompress_original_jpg_quality
450:         end
451:       )
452: 
453:     @upload.target_image_quality(@file.path, desired_quality).present?
454:   end

460:   def downsize!
461:     3.times do
462:       original_size = filesize
463:       down_tempfile = Tempfile.new(["down", ".#{@image_info.type}"])
464: 
465:       from = @file.path
466:       to = down_tempfile.path
467: 
468:       OptimizedImage.ensure_safe_paths!(from, to)
469: 
470:       OptimizedImage.downsize(from, to, "50%", scale_image: true, raise_on_error: true)
471: 
472:       @file.respond_to?(:close!) ? @file.close! : @file.close
473:       @file = down_tempfile
474: 
475:       extract_image_info!
476: 
477:       return if filesize >= original_size || pixels == 0 || !should_downsize?
478:     end
479:   rescue StandardError
480:     @upload.errors.add(:base, I18n.t("upload.optimize_failure_message"))
481:   end

499:           max_size: ActiveSupport::NumberHelper.number_to_human_size(max_image_size),
500:         ),
501:       )
502:       true

504:       false
505:     end

561:     case @opts[:type]

32:         adapter: options[:adapter],
33:       )

52:       end

54:       optimized_upload&.destroy
55:     end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module HasPostUploadReferences
4:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
5: 
6:   def link_post_uploads(fragments: nil)
7:     upload_ids = []
8: 
9:     each_upload_url(fragments: fragments) do |url, _, sha1|
10:       upload = Upload.fetch_from(sha1:, url:)
11: 
12:       # Link any video thumbnails
13:       if SiteSetting.video_thumbnails_enabled && upload.present? &&
14:            FileHelper.supported_video.include?(upload.extension&.downcase)
15:         # Video thumbnails have the filename of the video file sha1 with a .png or .jpg extension.
16:         # This is because at time of upload in the composer we don't know the topic/post id yet
17:         # and there is no thumbnail info added to the markdown to tie the thumbnail to the topic/post after
18:         # creation.
19:         thumbnail =
20:           Upload
21:             .where("original_filename like ?", "#{upload.sha1}.%")
22:             .order(id: :desc)
23:             .first if upload.sha1.present?
24:         if thumbnail.present?
25:           upload_ids << thumbnail.id
26:           handle_video_thumbnail(thumbnail) if respond_to?(:handle_video_thumbnail, true)
27:         end
28:       end
29:       upload_ids << upload.id if upload.present?
30:     end
31: 
32:     upload_references =
33:       upload_ids.map do |upload_id|
34:         {
35:           target_id: self.id,
36:           target_type: self.class.name,
37:           upload_id: upload_id,
38:           created_at: Time.zone.now,
39:           updated_at: Time.zone.now,
40:         }
41:       end
42: 
43:     UploadReference.transaction do
44:       UploadReference.where(target: self).delete_all
45:       UploadReference.insert_all(upload_references) if upload_references.size > 0
46: 
47:       if SiteSetting.secure_uploads?
48:         Upload
49:           .where(id: upload_ids, access_control_post_id: nil)
50:           .where("id NOT IN (SELECT upload_id FROM custom_emojis)")
51:           .update_all(access_control_post_id: access_control_post_id_for_upload)
52:       end
53:     end
54:   end
55: 
56:   def each_upload_url(fragments: nil, include_local_upload: true)
57:     current_db = RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.current_db
58: 
59:     upload_patterns = [
60:       %r{/uploads/#{current_db}/},
61:       %r{/original/},
62:       %r{/optimized/},
63:       %r{/uploads/short-url/[a-zA-Z0-9]+(\.[a-z0-9]+)?},
64:     ]
65: 
66:     fragments ||= Nokogiri::HTML5.fragment(self.cooked)
67: 
68:     selectors =
69:       fragments.css(
70:         "a/@href",
71:         "img/@src",
72:         "source/@src",
73:         "track/@src",
74:         "video/@poster",
75:         "div/@data-video-src",
76:         "div/@data-original-video-src",
77:       )
78: 
79:     links =
80:       selectors
81:         .map do |media|
82:           src = media.value
83:           next if src.blank?
84: 
85:           if src.end_with?("/images/transparent.png") &&
86:                (parent = media.parent)["data-orig-src"].present?
87:             parent["data-orig-src"]
88:           else
89:             src
90:           end
91:         end
92:         .compact
93:         .uniq
94: 
95:     links.each do |src|
96:       src = src.split("?")[0]
97: 
98:       if src.start_with?("upload://")
99:         sha1 = Upload.sha1_from_short_url(src)
100:         yield(src, nil, sha1)
101:         next
102:       end
103: 
104:       if src.include?("/uploads/short-url/")
105:         host =
106:           begin
107:             URI(src).host
108:           rescue URI::Error
109:           end
110: 
111:         next if host.present? && host != Discourse.current_hostname
112: 
113:         sha1 = Upload.sha1_from_short_path(src)
114:         yield(src, nil, sha1)
115:         next
116:       end
117: 
118:       next if upload_patterns.none? { |pattern| src =~ pattern }
119:       next if Rails.configuration.multisite && src.exclude?(current_db)
120: 
121:       src = "#{SiteSetting.force_https ? "https" : "http"}:#{src}" if src.start_with?("//")
122: 
123:       if !Discourse.store.has_been_uploaded?(src) && !Upload.secure_uploads_url?(src) &&
124:            !(include_local_upload && src =~ %r{\A/[^/]}i)
125:         next
126:       end
127: 
128:       path =
129:         begin
130:           URI(
131:             UrlHelper.unencode(GlobalSetting.cdn_url ? src.sub(GlobalSetting.cdn_url, "") : src),
132:           )&.path
133:         rescue URI::Error
134:         end
135: 
136:       next if path.blank?
137: 
138:       sha1 =
139:         if path.include? "optimized"
140:           OptimizedImage.extract_sha1(path)
141:         else
142:           Upload.extract_sha1(path) || Upload.sha1_from_short_path(path)
143:         end
144: 
145:       yield(src, path, sha1)
146:     end
147:   end
148: end

124:     def get_path_for_upload(upload)
125:       # try to extract the path from the URL instead of calculating it,
126:       # because the calculated path might differ from the actual path
127:       if upload.url.present? && (path = upload.url[UPLOAD_PATH_REGEX, 1])
128:         return prefix_path(path)
129:       end
130: 
131:       extension =
132:         if upload.extension
133:           ".#{upload.extension}"
134:         else
135:           # Maintain backward compatibility before Jobs::MigrateUploadExtensions runs
136:           File.extname(upload.original_filename)
137:         end
138: 
139:       get_path_for("original", upload.id, upload.sha1, extension)
140:     end
141: 
142:     def get_path_for_optimized_image(optimized_image)
143:       # try to extract the path from the URL instead of calculating it,
144:       # because the calculated path might differ from the actual path
145:       if optimized_image.url.present? && (path = optimized_image.url[OPTIMIZED_IMAGE_PATH_REGEX, 1])
146:         return prefix_path(path)
147:       end
148: 
149:       upload = optimized_image.upload
150:       version = optimized_image.version || 1
151:       extension =
152:         "_#{version}_#{optimized_image.width}x#{optimized_image.height}#{optimized_image.extension}"
153:       get_path_for("optimized", upload.id, upload.sha1, extension)
154:     end
155: 
156:     CACHE_DIR = "#{Rails.root}/tmp/download_cache/"
157:     CACHE_MAXIMUM_SIZE = 500
158:     CACHE_EVICT_COUNT = 100
159: 
160:     def get_cache_path_for(filename)
161:       "#{CACHE_DIR}#{filename}"
162:     end
163: 
164:     def get_from_cache(filename)
165:       path = get_cache_path_for(filename)
166:       path if File.exist?(path)
167:     end
168: 
169:     def cache_file(file, filename)
170:       path = get_cache_path_for(filename)
171:       dir = File.dirname(path)
172:       FileUtils.mkdir_p(dir) unless Dir.exist?(dir)
173:       FileUtils.cp(file.path, path)
174: 
175:       DiskCacheEviction.evict(
176:         dir: CACHE_DIR,
177:         max_entries: CACHE_MAXIMUM_SIZE,
178:         evict_count: CACHE_EVICT_COUNT,
179:       )
180:     end
181: 
182:     private
183: 
184:     def perform_download(object, max_file_size_kb: nil)
185:       DistributedMutex.synchronize("download_#{object.sha1}", validity: 3.minutes) do
186:         name = object.respond_to?(:original_filename) ? object.original_filename : object.url
187:         extension = File.extname(name)
188:         filename = "#{object.sha1}#{extension}"
189: 
190:         unless File.exist?(get_cache_path_for(filename))
191:           max_file_size_kb ||= [
192:             SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb,
193:             SiteSetting.max_attachment_size_kb,
194:           ].max.kilobytes
195: 
196:           if object.respond_to?(:secure) ? object.secure? : object.upload.secure?
197:             url =
198:               Discourse.store.signed_url_for_path(object.url, include_content_disposition: false)
199:           else
200:             url = Discourse.store.cdn_url(object.url)
201:           end
202: 
203:           url = "#{SiteSetting.scheme}:#{url}" if url =~ %r{\A//}
204: 
205:           file =
206:             FileHelper.download(
207:               url,
208:               max_file_size: max_file_size_kb,
209:               tmp_file_name: "discourse-download",
210:               follow_redirect: true,
211:             )
212: 
213:           return if file.nil?
214: 
215:           cache_file(file, filename)
216:         end
217: 
218:         get_from_cache(filename)
219:       end
220:     end
221: 
222:     def not_implemented
223:       raise "Not implemented."
224:     end
225: 
226:     def get_depth_for(id)
227:       depths = [0]
228:       depths << Math.log(id / 1_000.0, 16).ceil if id.positive?
229:       depths.max
230:     end
231: 
232:     def prefix_path(path)
233:       path
234:     end
235:   end
236: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module BackupRestore
4:   UploadsRestoreError = Class.new(RuntimeError)
5: 
6:   class UploadsRestorer
7:     delegate :log, to: :@logger, private: true
8: 
9:     S3_ENDPOINT_REGEX = /\.s3(?:\.dualstack\.[a-z0-9\-]+?|[.\-][a-z0-9\-]+?)?\.amazonaws\.com/
10: 
11:     def self.s3_regex_string(s3_base_url)
12:       clean_url = s3_base_url.sub(S3_ENDPOINT_REGEX, ".s3.amazonaws.com")
13: 
14:       regex_string =
15:         clean_url
16:           .split(".s3.amazonaws.com")
17:           .map { |s| Regexp.escape(s) }
18:           .insert(1, S3_ENDPOINT_REGEX.source)
19:           .join("")
20: 
21:       [regex_string, clean_url]
22:     end
23: 
24:     def initialize(logger)
25:       @logger = logger
26:     end
27: 
28:     def restore(tmp_directory)
29:       upload_directories =
30:         Dir
31:           .glob(File.join(tmp_directory, "uploads", "*"))
32:           .reject { |path| File.basename(path).start_with?("PaxHeaders") }
33: 
34:       if upload_directories.count > 1
35:         raise UploadsRestoreError.new(
36:                 "Could not find uploads, because the uploads " \
37:                   "directory contains multiple folders.",
38:               )
39:       end
40: 
41:       @tmp_uploads_path = upload_directories.first
42:       return if @tmp_uploads_path.blank?
43: 
44:       @previous_db_name = BackupMetadata.value_for("db_name") || File.basename(@tmp_uploads_path)
45:       @current_db_name = RailsMultisite::ConnectionManagement.current_db
46:       backup_contains_optimized_images = File.exist?(File.join(@tmp_uploads_path, "optimized"))
47: 
48:       remap_uploads
49:       restore_uploads
50: 
51:       generate_optimized_images unless backup_contains_optimized_images
52:       rebake_posts_with_uploads
53:     end
54: 
55:     protected
56: 
57:     def restore_uploads
58:       store = Discourse.store
59: 
60:       if !store.respond_to?(:copy_from)
61:         # a FileStore implementation from a plugin might not support this method, so raise a helpful error
62:         store_name = Discourse.store.class.name
63:         raise UploadsRestoreError.new(
64:                 "The current file store (#{store_name}) does not support restoring uploads.",
65:               )
66:       end
67: 
68:       log "Restoring uploads, this may take a while..."
69:       store.copy_from(@tmp_uploads_path)
70:     end
71: 
72:     # Remaps upload URLs depending on old and new configuration.
73:     # URLs of uploads differ a little bit between local uploads and uploads stored on S3.
74:     # Multisites are another reason why URLs can be different.
75:     #
76:     # Examples:
77:     #   * regular site, local storage
78:     #     /uploads/default/original/1X/63b76551662ccea1a594e161c37dd35188d77657.jpeg
79:     #
80:     #   * regular site, S3
81:     #     //bucket-name.s3.dualstack.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/original/1X/63b76551662ccea1a594e161c37dd35188d77657.jpeg
82:     #
83:     #   * multisite, local storage
84:     #     /uploads/<site-name>/original/1X/63b76551662ccea1a594e161c37dd35188d77657.jpeg
85:     #
86:     #   * multisite, S3
87:     #     //bucket-name.s3.dualstack.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/uploads/<site-name>/original/1X/63b76551662ccea1a594e161c37dd35188d77657.jpeg
88:     def remap_uploads
89:       log "Remapping uploads..."
90: 
91:       was_multisite = BackupMetadata.value_for("multisite") == "t"
92:       upload_path = "/#{Discourse.store.upload_path}/"
93:       uploads_folder = was_multisite ? "/" : upload_path
94: 
95:       if (old_base_url = BackupMetadata.value_for("base_url")) && old_base_url != Discourse.base_url
96:         remap(old_base_url, Discourse.base_url)
97:       end
98: 
99:       current_s3_base_url =
100:         SiteSetting::Upload.enable_s3_uploads ? SiteSetting::Upload.s3_base_url : nil
101:       if (old_s3_base_url = BackupMetadata.value_for("s3_base_url")) &&
102:            old_s3_base_url != current_s3_base_url
103:         remap_s3("#{old_s3_base_url}/", uploads_folder)
104:       end
105: 
106:       current_s3_cdn_url =
107:         SiteSetting::Upload.enable_s3_uploads ? SiteSetting::Upload.s3_cdn_url : nil
108:       if (old_s3_cdn_url = BackupMetadata.value_for("s3_cdn_url")) &&
109:            old_s3_cdn_url != current_s3_cdn_url
110:         base_url = current_s3_cdn_url || Discourse.base_url
111:         remap("#{old_s3_cdn_url}/", UrlHelper.schemaless("#{base_url}#{uploads_folder}"))
112: 
113:         old_host = URI.parse(old_s3_cdn_url).host
114:         new_host = URI.parse(base_url).host
115:         remap(old_host, new_host) if old_host != new_host
116:       end
117: 
118:       if (old_cdn_url = BackupMetadata.value_for("cdn_url")) && old_cdn_url != Discourse.asset_host
119:         base_url = Discourse.asset_host || Discourse.base_url
120:         remap("#{old_cdn_url}/", UrlHelper.schemaless("#{base_url}/"))
121: 
122:         old_host = URI.parse(old_cdn_url).host
123:         new_host = URI.parse(base_url).host
124:         remap(old_host, new_host) if old_host != new_host
125:       end
126: 
127:       remap("/uploads/#{@previous_db_name}/", upload_path) if @previous_db_name != @current_db_name
128:     rescue => ex
129:       log "Something went wrong while remapping uploads.", ex
130:     end
131: 
132:     def remap(from, to)
133:       log "Remapping '#{from}' to '#{to}'"
134:       DbHelper.remap(from, to, verbose: true, excluded_tables: ["backup_metadata"])
135:     end
136: 
137:     def remap_s3(old_s3_base_url, uploads_folder)
138:       if old_s3_base_url.include?("amazonaws.com")
139:         from_regex, from_clean_url = self.class.s3_regex_string(old_s3_base_url)
140:         log "Remapping with regex from '#{from_clean_url}' to '#{uploads_folder}'"
141:         DbHelper.regexp_replace(
142:           from_regex,
143:           uploads_folder,
144:           verbose: true,
145:           excluded_tables: ["backup_metadata"],
146:         )
147:       else
148:         remap(old_s3_base_url, uploads_folder)
149:       end
150:     end
151: 
152:     def generate_optimized_images
153:       log "Optimizing site icons..."
154:       DB.exec("TRUNCATE TABLE optimized_images")
155:       SiteIconManager.ensure_optimized!
156: 
157:       User
158:         .where.not(uploaded_avatar_id: nil)
159:         .find_each do |user|
160:           Jobs.enqueue(:create_avatar_thumbnails, upload_id: user.uploaded_avatar_id)
161:         end
162:     end
163: 
164:     def rebake_posts_with_uploads
165:       log "Posts will be rebaked by a background job in sidekiq. You will see missing images until that has completed."
166:       log 'You can expedite the process by manually running "rake posts:rebake_uncooked_posts"'
167: 
168:       DB.exec(<<~SQL)

170:         SET baked_version = NULL
171:         WHERE id IN (SELECT target_id FROM upload_references WHERE target_type = 'Post')

173:     end
174:   end

3: class UserAvatar < ActiveRecord::Base
4:   belongs_to :user
5:   belongs_to :gravatar_upload, class_name: "Upload"
6:   belongs_to :custom_upload, class_name: "Upload"
7:   has_many :upload_references, as: :target, dependent: :destroy
8: 
9:   after_save do
10:     if saved_change_to_custom_upload_id? || saved_change_to_gravatar_upload_id?
11:       upload_ids = [self.custom_upload_id, self.gravatar_upload_id]
12:       UploadReference.ensure_exist!(upload_ids: upload_ids, target: self)
13:     end
14:   end
15: 
16:   @@custom_user_gravatar_email_hash = {
17:     Discourse::SYSTEM_USER_ID => User.email_hash("info@discourse.org"),
18:   }
19: 
20:   def self.register_custom_user_gravatar_email_hash(user_id, email)
21:     @@custom_user_gravatar_email_hash[user_id] = User.email_hash(email)
22:   end
23: 
24:   def contains_upload?(id)
25:     gravatar_upload_id == id || custom_upload_id == id
26:   end
27: 
28:   def update_gravatar!
29:     DistributedMutex.synchronize("update_gravatar_#{user_id}") do
30:       begin
31:         self.update!(last_gravatar_download_attempt: Time.zone.now)
32: 
33:         max = Discourse.avatar_sizes.max
34: 
35:         # The user could be deleted before this executes
36:         return if user.blank? || user.primary_email.blank?
37: 
38:         email_hash = @@custom_user_gravatar_email_hash[user_id] || user.email_hash
39:         gravatar_url =
40:           "https://#{SiteSetting.gravatar_base_url}/avatar/#{email_hash}.png?s=#{max}&d=404&reset_cache=#{SecureRandom.urlsafe_base64(5)}"
41: 
42:         if SiteSetting.verbose_upload_logging
43:           Rails.logger.warn("Verbose Upload Logging: Downloading gravatar from #{gravatar_url}")
44:         end
45: 
46:         # follow redirects in case gravatar change rules on us
47:         tempfile =
48:           FileHelper.download(
49:             gravatar_url,
50:             max_file_size: SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb.kilobytes,
51:             tmp_file_name: "gravatar",
52:             skip_rate_limit: true,
53:             verbose: false,
54:             follow_redirect: true,
55:           )
56: 
57:         if tempfile
58:           ext = File.extname(tempfile)
59:           ext = ".png" if ext.blank?
60: 
61:           upload =
62:             UploadCreator.new(
63:               tempfile,
64:               "gravatar#{ext}",
65:               origin: gravatar_url,
66:               type: "avatar",
67:               for_gravatar: true,
68:             ).create_for(user_id)
69: 
70:           if gravatar_upload_id != upload.id
71:             User.transaction do
72:               if gravatar_upload_id && user.uploaded_avatar_id == gravatar_upload_id
73:                 user.update!(uploaded_avatar_id: upload.id)
74:               end
75: 
76:               self.update!(gravatar_upload: upload)
77:             end
78:           end
79:         end
80:       rescue OpenURI::HTTPError => e
81:         raise e if e.io&.status&.[](0).to_i != 404
82:       ensure
83:         tempfile&.close!
84:       end
85:     end
86:   end
87: 
88:   def self.local_avatar_url(hostname, username, upload_id, size)
89:     self.local_avatar_template(hostname, username, upload_id).gsub("{size}", size.to_s)
90:   end
91: 
92:   def self.local_avatar_template(hostname, username, upload_id)
93:     version = self.version(upload_id)
94:     "#{Discourse.base_path}/user_avatar/#{hostname}/#{username}/{size}/#{version}.png"
95:   end
96: 
97:   def self.external_avatar_url(user_id, upload_id, size)
98:     self.external_avatar_template(user_id, upload_id).gsub("{size}", size.to_s)
99:   end
100: 
101:   def self.external_avatar_template(user_id, upload_id)
102:     version = self.version(upload_id)
103:     "#{Discourse.store.absolute_base_url}/avatars/#{user_id}/{size}/#{version}.png"
104:   end
105: 
106:   def self.version(upload_id)
107:     "#{upload_id}_#{OptimizedImage::VERSION}"
108:   end
109: 
110:   def self.import_url_for_user(avatar_url, user, options = nil)
111:     if SiteSetting.verbose_upload_logging
112:       Rails.logger.warn("Verbose Upload Logging: Downloading sso-avatar from #{avatar_url}")
113:     end
114: 
115:     tempfile =
116:       FileHelper.download(
117:         avatar_url,
118:         max_file_size: SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb.kilobytes,
119:         tmp_file_name: "sso-avatar",
120:         follow_redirect: true,
121:         skip_rate_limit: !!options&.fetch(:skip_rate_limit, false),
122:       )
123: 
124:     return unless tempfile
125: 
126:     ext = FastImage.type(tempfile).to_s
127:     tempfile.rewind
128: 
129:     upload =
130:       UploadCreator.new(
131:         tempfile,
132:         "external-avatar." + ext,
133:         origin: avatar_url,
134:         type: "avatar",
135:       ).create_for(user.id)
136: 
137:     user.create_user_avatar! unless user.user_avatar
138: 
139:     if !user.user_avatar.contains_upload?(upload.id)
140:       user.user_avatar.update!(custom_upload_id: upload.id)
141:       override_gravatar = !options || options[:override_gravatar]
142: 
143:       if user.uploaded_avatar_id.nil? ||
144:            !user.user_avatar.contains_upload?(user.uploaded_avatar_id) || override_gravatar
145:         user.update!(uploaded_avatar_id: upload.id)
146:       end
147:     end
148:   rescue Net::ReadTimeout, OpenURI::HTTPError, FinalDestination::SSRFError
149:     # Skip saving. We are not connected to the net, or SSRF checks failed.
150:   ensure
151:     tempfile.close! if tempfile && tempfile.respond_to?(:close!)
152:   end
153: 
154:   def self.ensure_consistency!(max_optimized_avatars_to_remove: 20_000)
155:     DB.exec <<~SQL
156:       DELETE FROM user_avatars
157:       USING user_avatars ua
158:       LEFT JOIN users u ON ua.user_id = u.id
159:       WHERE user_avatars.id = ua.id AND u.id IS NULL
160:     SQL
161: 
162:     DB.exec <<~SQL
163:       UPDATE user_avatars
164:       SET gravatar_upload_id = NULL
165:       WHERE gravatar_upload_id IN (
166:         SELECT u1.gravatar_upload_id FROM user_avatars u1
167:         LEFT JOIN uploads up
168:           ON u1.gravatar_upload_id = up.id
169:         WHERE u1.gravatar_upload_id IS NOT NULL AND
170:           up.id IS NULL
171:       )
172:     SQL
173: 
174:     DB.exec <<~SQL
175:       UPDATE user_avatars
176:       SET custom_upload_id = NULL
177:       WHERE custom_upload_id IN (
178:         SELECT u1.custom_upload_id FROM user_avatars u1
179:         LEFT JOIN uploads up
180:           ON u1.custom_upload_id = up.id
181:         WHERE u1.custom_upload_id IS NOT NULL AND
182:           up.id IS NULL
183:       )
184:     SQL
185: 
186:     ids =
187:       DB.query_single(<<~SQL, sizes: Discourse.avatar_sizes, limit: max_optimized_avatars_to_remove)
188:       SELECT oi.id FROM (
189:         SELECT custom_upload_id FROM user_avatars
190:         EXCEPT
191:         SELECT upload_id FROM  upload_references WHERE target_type <> 'UserAvatar'
192:         AND upload_id IS NOT NULL
193:       ) AS a
194:       JOIN optimized_images oi ON oi.upload_id = a.custom_upload_id
195:       WHERE oi.width not in (:sizes) AND oi.height not in (:sizes)
196:       LIMIT :limit
197:     SQL
198: 
199:     warnings_reported = 0
200: 
201:     ids.each do |id|
202:       begin
203:         OptimizedImage.find(id).destroy!
204:       rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
205:       rescue => e
206:         if warnings_reported < 10
207:           Discourse.warn_exception(e, message: "Failed to remove optimized image")
208:           warnings_reported += 1
209:         end
210:       end
211:     end
212:   end
213: end
