1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class ThemeField < ActiveRecord::Base
4:   MIGRATION_NAME_PART_MAX_LENGTH = 150
5: 
6:   # This string is not 'secret'. It's just randomized to avoid accidental
7:   # clashes with genuine theme field content.
8:   CSP_NONCE_PLACEHOLDER = "__CSP__NONCE__PLACEHOLDER__f72bff1b1768168a34ee092ce759f192__"
9: 
10:   belongs_to :upload
11:   has_one :javascript_cache, -> { where(name: nil) }, dependent: :destroy, autosave: true
12:   has_many :raw_javascript_caches,
13:            -> { where.not(name: nil) },
14:            class_name: "JavascriptCache",
15:            dependent: :destroy,
16:            autosave: true
17:   has_one :upload_reference, as: :target, dependent: :destroy
18:   has_one :theme_settings_migration, dependent: :destroy
19: 
20:   validates :value, { length: { maximum: 1024**2 } }
21: 
22:   validate :migration_filename_is_valid, if: :migration_field?
23: 
24:   after_save do
25:     if (
26:          self.type_id == ThemeField.types[:theme_screenshot_upload_var] ||
27:            self.type_id == ThemeField.types[:theme_upload_var]
28:        ) && saved_change_to_upload_id?
29:       UploadReference.ensure_exist!(upload_ids: [self.upload_id], target: self)
30:     end
31:   end
32: 
33:   after_save do
34:     dependent_fields.each(&:invalidate_baked!)
35: 
36:     if upload && svg_sprite_field?
37:       upsert_svg_sprite!
38:       SvgSprite.expire_cache
39:     end
40:   end
41: 
42:   after_destroy do
43:     if svg_sprite_field?
44:       ThemeSvgSprite.where(theme_id: theme_id).delete_all
45: 
46:       SvgSprite.expire_cache
47:     end
48:   end
49: 
50:   scope :find_by_theme_ids,
51:         ->(theme_ids) do
52:           return none if theme_ids.blank?
53: 
54:           where(theme_id: theme_ids).joins(
55:             "JOIN (
56:           SELECT #{theme_ids.map.with_index { |id, idx| "#{id.to_i} AS theme_id, #{idx} AS theme_sort_column" }.join(" UNION ALL SELECT ")}
57:         ) as X ON X.theme_id = theme_fields.theme_id",
58:           ).order("theme_sort_column")
59:         end
60: 
61:   scope :filter_locale_fields,
62:         ->(locale_codes) do
63:           return none if locale_codes.blank?
64: 
65:           where(target_id: Theme.targets[:translations], name: locale_codes).joins(
66:             DB.sql_fragment(
67:               "JOIN (
68:         SELECT * FROM (VALUES #{locale_codes.map { "(?)" }.join(",")}) as Y (locale_code, locale_sort_column)
69:       ) as Y ON Y.locale_code = theme_fields.name",
70:               *locale_codes.map.with_index { |code, index| [code, index] },
71:             ),
72:           ).order("Y.locale_sort_column")
73:         end
74: 
75:   scope :find_first_locale_fields,
76:         ->(theme_ids, locale_codes) do
77:           find_by_theme_ids(theme_ids)
78:             .filter_locale_fields(locale_codes)
79:             .reorder("X.theme_sort_column", "Y.locale_sort_column")
80:             .select("DISTINCT ON (X.theme_sort_column) *")
81:         end
82: 
83:   scope :svg_sprite_fields,
84:         -> do
85:           where(type_id: ThemeField.theme_var_type_ids, name: SvgSprite.theme_sprite_variable_name)
86:         end
87: 
88:   def self.types
89:     @types ||=
90:       Enum.new(
91:         html: 0,
92:         scss: 1,
93:         theme_upload_var: 2,
94:         theme_color_var: 3, # No longer used
95:         theme_var: 4, # No longer used
96:         yaml: 5,
97:         js: 6,
98:         theme_screenshot_upload_var: 7,
99:         json: 8,
100:       )
101:   end
102: 
103:   def self.theme_var_type_ids
104:     @theme_var_type_ids ||= [2]
105:   end
106: 
107:   def self.css_theme_type_ids
108:     @css_theme_type_ids ||= [0, 1]
109:   end
110: 
111:   def self.force_recompilation!
112:     find_each do |field|
113:       field.compiler_version = 0
114:       field.ensure_baked!
115:     end
116:   end
117: 
118:   validates :name,
119:             format: {
120:               with: /\A[a-z_][a-z0-9_-]*\z/i,
121:             },
122:             if: Proc.new { |field| ThemeField.theme_var_type_ids.include?(field.type_id) }
123: 
124:   belongs_to :theme
125: 
126:   def process_html(html)
127:     errors = []
128:     errors << I18n.t("themes.errors.optimized_link") if contains_optimized_link?(html)
129: 
130:     doc = Nokogiri::HTML5.fragment(html)
131: 
132:     unused_raw_caches = Set.new raw_javascript_caches.to_a
133: 
134:     doc
135:       .css("script")
136:       .select { |node| inline_javascript?(node) }
137:       .each_with_index do |node, index|
138:         unique_name =
139:           "theme-#{theme_id}-inline-#{Theme.targets[self.target_id]}-#{name}#{"-#{index + 1}" if index > 0}"
140: 
141:         cache =
142:           raw_javascript_caches.find { |c| c.name == unique_name } ||
143:             raw_javascript_caches.build(name: unique_name)
144:         transpiled =
145:           begin
146:             AssetProcessor.new(skip_module: true).perform(
147:               node.inner_html,
148:               nil,
149:               "theme-#{theme_id}/#{unique_name}.js",
150:               generate_map: true,
151:             )
152:           rescue AssetProcessor::TranspileError => e
153:             message = "[THEME #{theme_id} '#{theme.name}'] Compile error: #{e.message}"
154:             errors << message
155:             { "code" => "console.error(#{message.to_json});\n", "map" => nil }
156:           end
157:         cache.content = transpiled["code"]
158:         cache.source_map = transpiled["map"]
159:         cache.save!
160:         unused_raw_caches.delete(cache)
161: 
162:         node.replace "<script defer src='#{cache.url}' data-theme-id='#{theme_id}'></script>"
163:       end
164: 
165:     doc.css("script").each { |node| node["nonce"] = CSP_NONCE_PLACEHOLDER }
166: 
167:     unused_raw_caches.each(&:destroy!)
168: 
169:     [doc.to_s, errors&.join("\n")]
170:   end
171: 
172:   def validate_svg_sprite_xml
173:     upload = Upload.find_by(id: self.upload_id)
174: 
175:     return "Error with #{self.name}: No upload found for the provided upload_id" if upload.nil?
176: 
177:     if Discourse.store.external?
178:       path = Discourse.store.download(upload)
179:     else
180:       path = Discourse.store.path_for(upload)
181:     end
182: 
183:     return "Error with #{self.name}: Could not download icon sprite" if path.nil?
184: 
185:     error = nil
186: 
187:     begin
188:       content = File.read(path)
189:       if content.to_s.bytesize > SvgSprite::MAX_THEME_SPRITE_SIZE
190:         error = "Error with #{self.name}: Icon sprite file is too large"
191:       else
192:         Nokogiri.XML(content) { |config| config.options = Nokogiri::XML::ParseOptions::NOBLANKS }
193:       end
194:     rescue => e
195:       error = "Error with #{self.name}: #{e.inspect}"
196:     end
197: 
198:     error
199:   end
200: 
201:   def raw_translation_data(internal: false)
202:     # Might raise ThemeTranslationParser::InvalidYaml
203:     ThemeTranslationParser.new(self, internal: internal).load
204:   end
205: 
206:   def translation_data(with_overrides: true, internal: false, fallback_fields: nil)
207:     fallback_fields ||= theme.theme_fields.filter_locale_fields(I18n.fallbacks[name])
208: 
209:     fallback_data =
210:       fallback_fields.each_with_index.map do |field, index|
211:         begin
212:           field.raw_translation_data(internal: internal)
213:         rescue ThemeTranslationParser::InvalidYaml
214:           # If this is the locale with the error, raise it.
215:           # If not, let the other theme_field raise the error when it processes itself
216:           raise if field.id == id
217:           {}
218:         end
219:       end
220: 
221:     # TODO: Deduplicate the fallback data in the same way as JSLocaleHelper#load_translations_merged
222:     #       this would reduce the size of the payload, without affecting functionality
223:     data = {}
224:     fallback_data.each { |hash| data.merge!(hash) }
225: 
226:     if with_overrides
227:       overrides = theme.translation_override_hash.deep_symbolize_keys
228:       data.deep_merge!(overrides)
229:     end
230: 
231:     data
232:   end
233: 
234:   def process_translation
235:     errors = []
236:     javascript_cache || build_javascript_cache
237: 
238:     data = translation_data
239: 
240:     js = <<~JS
241:       /* Translation data for theme #{self.theme_id} (#{self.name})*/
242:       const localeData = window._discourse_locale_data ??= {};
243:       localeData.theme ??= {};
244:       localeData.theme[#{self.theme_id}] = #{data.to_json};
245:     JS
246: 
247:     javascript_cache.content = js
248:     javascript_cache.source_map = nil
249:     javascript_cache.save!
250: 
251:     doc = ""
252:     doc = <<~HTML.html_safe if javascript_cache.content.present?
253:           <script type="module" src="#{javascript_cache.url}" data-theme-id="#{theme_id}" nonce="#{ThemeField::CSP_NONCE_PLACEHOLDER}"></script>
254:         HTML
255:     [doc, errors&.join("\n")]
256:   rescue ThemeTranslationParser::InvalidYaml => e
257:     ["", e.message]
258:   end
259: 
260:   def validate_yaml!
261:     return unless self.name == "yaml"
262: 
263:     errors = []
264: 
265:     begin
266:       ThemeSettingsParser
267:         .new(self)
268:         .load do |name, default, type, opts|
269:           setting = ThemeSetting.new(name: name, data_type: type, theme: theme)
270:           translation_key = "themes.settings_errors"
271: 
272:           if setting.invalid?
273:             setting.errors.details.each_pair do |attribute, _errors|
274:               _errors.each do |hash|
275:                 errors << I18n.t("#{translation_key}.#{attribute}_#{hash[:error]}", name: name)
276:               end
277:             end
278:           end
279: 
280:           unless ThemeSettingsValidator.is_value_present?(default)
281:             errors << I18n.t("#{translation_key}.default_value_missing", name: name)
282:             next
283:           end
284: 
285:           unless ThemeSettingsValidator.is_valid_value_type?(default, type)
286:             errors << I18n.t("#{translation_key}.default_not_match_type", name: name)
287:           end
288: 
289:           if (setting_errors = ThemeSettingsValidator.validate_value(default, type, opts)).present?
290:             errors << I18n.t(
291:               "#{translation_key}.default_value_not_valid",
292:               name: name,
293:               error_messages: setting_errors.join(" "),
294:             )
295:           end
296:         end
297:     rescue ThemeSettingsParser::InvalidYaml => e
298:       errors << e.message
299:     end
300: 
301:     self.error = errors.join("\n").presence
302:   end
303: 
304:   def self.guess_type(name:, target:)
305:     if basic_targets.include?(target.to_s) && html_fields.include?(name.to_s)
306:       types[:html]
307:     elsif basic_targets.include?(target.to_s) && scss_fields.include?(name.to_s)
308:       types[:scss]
309:     elsif target.to_s == "extra_scss"
310:       types[:scss]
311:     elsif %w[migrations extra_js].include?(target.to_s)
312:       types[:js]
313:     elsif target.to_s == "settings" || target.to_s == "translations"
314:       types[:yaml]
315:     end
316:   end
317: 
318:   def self.html_fields
319:     @html_fields ||= %w[body_tag head_tag header footer after_header embedded_header]
320:   end
321: 
322:   def self.scss_fields
323:     @scss_fields ||= %w[scss embedded_scss color_definitions]
324:   end
325: 
326:   def self.basic_targets
327:     @basic_targets ||= %w[common desktop mobile]
328:   end
329: 
330:   def basic_html_field?
331:     ThemeField.basic_targets.include?(Theme.targets[self.target_id].to_s) &&
332:       ThemeField.html_fields.include?(self.name)
333:   end
334: 
335:   def extra_js_field?
336:     Theme.targets[self.target_id] == :extra_js
337:   end
338: 
339:   def js_tests_field?
340:     Theme.targets[self.target_id] == :tests_js
341:   end
342: 
343:   def basic_scss_field?
344:     ThemeField.basic_targets.include?(Theme.targets[self.target_id].to_s) &&
345:       ThemeField.scss_fields.include?(self.name)
346:   end
347: 
348:   def extra_scss_field?
349:     Theme.targets[self.target_id] == :extra_scss
350:   end
351: 
352:   def settings_field?
353:     Theme.targets[:settings] == self.target_id
354:   end
355: 
356:   def translation_field?
357:     Theme.targets[:translations] == self.target_id
358:   end
359: 
360:   def svg_sprite_field?
361:     ThemeField.theme_var_type_ids.include?(self.type_id) &&
362:       self.name == SvgSprite.theme_sprite_variable_name
363:   end
364: 
365:   def migration_field?
366:     Theme.targets[:migrations] == self.target_id
367:   end
368: 
369:   def ensure_baked!
370:     needs_baking = !self.value_baked || compiler_version != Theme.compiler_version
371:     return unless needs_baking
372: 
373:     if basic_html_field? || translation_field?
374:       self.value_baked, self.error =
375:         translation_field? ? process_translation : process_html(self.value)
376:       self.error = nil if self.error.blank?

489:     # regex: used to match file names to fields (import).
490:     #        can contain named capture groups for name/type/target
491:     # canonical: a lambda which converts name/type/target
492:     #            to filename (export)
493:     # targets/names/types: can be nil if any value is allowed
494:     #                          single value
495:     #                          array of allowed values
496:     def initialize(regex:, canonical:, targets:, names:, types:)
497:       @allowed_values = {}
498:       @allowed_values[:names] = Array(names) if names
499:       @allowed_values[:targets] = Array(targets) if targets
500:       @allowed_values[:types] = Array(types) if types
501:       @canonical = canonical
502:       @regex = regex
503:     end
504: 
505:     def opts_from_filename(filename)
506:       match = @regex.match(filename)
507:       return false unless match
508:       hash = {}
509:       OPTIONS.each do |option|
510:         plural = :"#{option}s"
511:         hash[option] = @allowed_values[plural][0] if @allowed_values[plural]&.length == 1
512:         hash[option] = match[option] if hash[option].nil?
513:       end
514:       hash
515:     end

521:           next true if @allowed_values[plural] == nil # Allows any value
522:           next true if @allowed_values[plural].include?(opts[option]) # Value is allowed
523:         end
524:       is_match ? @canonical.call(opts) : nil
525:     end
526:   end

41:     def lookup_upload_urls(urls)
42:       map = {}
43:       result = {}
44: 
45:       urls.each do |url|
46:         sha1 = Upload.sha1_from_short_url(url)
47:         if (url.split(".")[1].nil?) # video sha1 without extension for thumbnail
48:           thumbnail = Upload.where("original_filename LIKE ?", "#{sha1}.%").last if sha1
49:           # Fallback for old posts that don't contain data-video-base62-sha1
50:           thumbnail = Upload.where("original_filename LIKE ?", "#{url}.%").last if thumbnail.nil? &&
51:             sha1.nil?
52:           sha1 = thumbnail.sha1 if thumbnail
53:         end
54:         map[url] = sha1 if sha1
55:       end
56: 
57:       if map.length > 0
58:         reverse_map = {}
59: 
60:         map.each do |key, value|
61:           reverse_map[value] ||= []
62:           reverse_map[value] << key
63:         end
64: 
65:         Upload
66:           .where(sha1: map.values)
67:           .pluck(:sha1, :url, :extension, :secure)
68:           .each do |row|
69:             sha1, url, extension, secure = row
70: 
71:             if short_urls = reverse_map[sha1]
72:               secure_uploads = SiteSetting.secure_uploads? && secure
73: 
74:               short_urls.each do |short_url|
75:                 result[short_url] = {
76:                   url:
77:                     (
78:                       if secure_uploads
79:                         Upload.secure_uploads_url_from_upload_url(url)
80:                       else
81:                         Discourse.store.cdn_url(url)
82:                       end
83:                     ),
84:                   short_path: Upload.short_path(sha1: sha1, extension: extension),
85:                   base62_sha1: Upload.base62_sha1(sha1),
86:                 }
87:               end
88:             end
89:           end
90:       end
91: 
92:       result
93:     end

288:   def update_muted_users(usernames)
289:     usernames ||= ""
290:     desired_usernames = usernames.split(",").reject { |username| user.username == username }
291:     desired_ids = User.where(username: desired_usernames).pluck(:id)
292:     if desired_ids.empty?
293:       MutedUser.where(user_id: user.id).destroy_all
294:     else
295:       MutedUser.where("user_id = ? AND muted_user_id not in (?)", user.id, desired_ids).destroy_all
296: 
297:       # SQL is easier here than figuring out how to do the same in AR
298:       DB.exec(<<~SQL, now: Time.now, user_id: user.id, desired_ids: desired_ids)
299:         INSERT into muted_users(user_id, muted_user_id, created_at, updated_at)
300:         SELECT :user_id, id, :now, :now
301:         FROM users
302:         WHERE id in (:desired_ids)
303:         ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
304:       SQL
305:     end
306:   end
307: 
308:   def update_allowed_pm_users(usernames)
309:     usernames ||= ""
310:     desired_usernames = usernames.split(",").reject { |username| user.username == username }
311:     desired_ids = User.where(username: desired_usernames).pluck(:id)
312: 
313:     if desired_ids.empty?
314:       AllowedPmUser.where(user_id: user.id).destroy_all
315:     else
316:       AllowedPmUser.where(
317:         "user_id = ? AND allowed_pm_user_id not in (?)",
318:         user.id,
319:         desired_ids,
320:       ).destroy_all
321: 
322:       # SQL is easier here than figuring out how to do the same in AR
323:       DB.exec(<<~SQL, now: Time.zone.now, user_id: user.id, desired_ids: desired_ids)
324:         INSERT into allowed_pm_users(user_id, allowed_pm_user_id, created_at, updated_at)
325:         SELECT :user_id, id, :now, :now
326:         FROM users
327:         WHERE id in (:desired_ids)
328:         ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
329:       SQL
330:     end
331:   end

3: class SiteSetting::Update
4:   include Service::Base

6:   Setting = Struct.new(:name, :value, :backfill, :change)
7: 
8:   options do
9:     attribute :allow_changing_hidden, :array, default: []
10:     attribute :overridden_setting_names, default: {}
11:   end
12: 
13:   policy :current_user_is_admin
14: 
15:   params do
16:     attribute :settings
17: 
18:     before_validation do
19:       dependent_order = SiteSetting.type_supervisor.dependencies.order
20: 
21:       self.settings =
22:         self
23:           .settings
24:           .to_a
25:           .map do |setting|
26:             Setting.new(
27:               setting[:setting_name].to_sym,
28:               setting[:value].to_s.strip,
29:               !!setting[:backfill],
30:               nil,
31:             )
32:           end
33:           .sort_by { |s| dependent_order.index(s.name) }
34:     end
35: 
36:     validates :settings, presence: true
37: 
38:     after_validation do
39:       self.settings =
40:         self.settings.map do |setting|
41:           raw_value = setting.value
42: 
43:           setting.value =
44:             case SiteSetting.type_supervisor.get_type(setting.name)
45:             when :integer
46:               raw_value.tr("^-0-9", "").to_i
47:             when :file_size_restriction
48:               raw_value.tr("^0-9", "").to_i
49:             when :uploaded_image_list
50:               raw_value.blank? ? "" : Upload.get_from_urls(raw_value.split("|")).to_a
51:             when :upload
52:               Upload.get_from_url(raw_value) || ""
53:             else
54:               raw_value
55:             end
56: 
57:           # Make sure to get the correct value here based on type, otherwise
58:           # we can end up inserting a staff action log when e.g. setting the value
59:           # to "false" when the setting value is already false.
60:           setting.value = SiteSetting.type_supervisor.to_rb_value(setting.name, setting.value)
61: 
62:           setting
63:         end
64:     end
65:   end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Jobs
4:   # Sometimes we need to update the access control metadata for a _lot_ of objects on S3 (such as when secure uploads
5:   # is enabled), this is best spread out over many jobs instead of having to do the whole thing serially.
6:   class SyncAccessControlForUploads < ::Jobs::Base
7:     sidekiq_options queue: "low"
8: 
9:     def execute(args)
10:       return if !Discourse.store.external?
11:       return if !args.key?(:upload_ids)
12: 
13:       Upload
14:         .includes(:optimized_images)
15:         .where(id: args[:upload_ids])
16:         .find_in_batches do |uploads|
17:           uploads.each do |upload|
18:             begin
19:               Discourse.store.update_upload_access_control(upload, remove_existing_acl: true)
20:             rescue => err
21:               Discourse.warn_exception(
22:                 err,
23:                 message: "Failed to update upload access control",
24:                 env: {
25:                   upload_id: upload.id,
26:                   filename: upload.original_filename,
27:                 },
28:               )
29:             end
30:           end
31:         end
32:     end
33:   end
34: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Jobs
4:   class CleanUpUploads < ::Jobs::Scheduled
5:     every 1.hour
6: 
7:     def execute(args)
8:       grace_period = [SiteSetting.clean_orphan_uploads_grace_period_hours, 1].max
9: 
10:       # Always remove invalid upload records regardless of clean_up_uploads setting.
11:       Upload
12:         .by_users
13:         .where(
14:           "retain_hours IS NULL OR created_at < current_timestamp - interval '1 hour' * retain_hours",
15:         )
16:         .where("created_at < ?", grace_period.hour.ago)
17:         .where(url: "")
18:         .find_each(&:destroy!)
19: 
20:       return unless SiteSetting.clean_up_uploads?
21: 
22:       # Do nothing if the last cleanup was run too recently.
23:       last_cleanup_timestamp = last_cleanup
24:       if last_cleanup_timestamp.present? &&
25:            (Time.zone.now.to_i - last_cleanup_timestamp) < (grace_period / 2).hours
26:         return
27:       end
28: 
29:       result = Upload.by_users
30:       Upload.unused_callbacks&.each { |handler| result = handler.call(result) }
31: 
32:       # 1. Exclude uploads that have retain_hours set and are still within that retention period.
33:       # 2. Exclude uploads created in the grace period.
34:       # 3. Exclude secure uploads that have an access_control_post_id.
35:       # 4. Exclude uploads that are link to an upload reference.
36:       # 5. Exclude uploads that are linked to anything but a Post via UploadReference.
37:       result =
38:         result
39:           .where(
40:             "uploads.retain_hours IS NULL OR uploads.created_at < current_timestamp - interval '1 hour' * uploads.retain_hours",
41:           )
42:           .where("uploads.created_at < ?", grace_period.hour.ago)
43:           .where(
44:             "((uploads.access_control_post_id IS NULL) OR (uploads.access_control_post_id IS NOT NULL AND NOT uploads.secure))",
45:           )
46:           .joins("LEFT JOIN upload_references ON upload_references.upload_id = uploads.id")
47:           .where("upload_references.upload_id IS NULL")
48:           .with_no_non_post_relations
49: 
50:       result.find_each do |upload|
51:         next if Upload.in_use_callbacks&.any? { |callback| callback.call(upload) }
52:         upload.sha1.present? ? upload.destroy : upload.delete
53:       end
54: 
55:       ExternalUploadStub.cleanup!
56: 
57:       self.last_cleanup = Time.zone.now.to_i
58:     end
59: 
60:     def last_cleanup=(timestamp)
61:       Discourse.redis.setex(last_cleanup_key, 7.days.to_i, timestamp.to_s)
62:     end
63: 
64:     def last_cleanup
65:       timestamp = Discourse.redis.get(last_cleanup_key)
66:       timestamp ? timestamp.to_i : timestamp
67:     end
68: 
69:     def reset_last_cleanup!
70:       Discourse.redis.del(last_cleanup_key)
71:     end
72: 
73:     protected
74: 
75:     def last_cleanup_key
76:       "LAST_UPLOAD_CLEANUP"
77:     end
78:   end
79: end

189:   def upload_backup_chunk
190:     filename = params.fetch(:resumableFilename)
191:     total_size = params.fetch(:resumableTotalSize).to_i
192:     identifier = params.fetch(:resumableIdentifier)
193: 
194:     raise Discourse::InvalidParameters.new(:resumableIdentifier) unless valid_filename?(identifier)
195:     unless valid_extension?(filename)
196:       return(
197:         render status: :unsupported_media_type, plain: I18n.t("backup.backup_file_should_be_tar_gz")
198:       )
199:     end
200:     unless has_enough_space_on_disk?(total_size)
201:       return(
202:         render status: :unsupported_media_type, plain: I18n.t("backup.not_enough_space_on_disk")
203:       )
204:     end
205:     unless valid_filename?(filename)
206:       return render status: :unsupported_media_type, plain: I18n.t("backup.invalid_filename")
207:     end
208: 
209:     file = params.fetch(:file)
210:     chunk_number = params.fetch(:resumableChunkNumber).to_i
211:     chunk_size = params.fetch(:resumableChunkSize).to_i
212:     current_chunk_size = params.fetch(:resumableCurrentChunkSize).to_i
213:     previous_chunk_number = chunk_number - 1
214: 
215:     chunk = BackupRestore::LocalBackupStore.chunk_path(identifier, filename, chunk_number)
216:     HandleChunkUpload.upload_chunk(chunk, file: file)
217: 
218:     # when all chunks are uploaded
219:     uploaded_file_size = previous_chunk_number * chunk_size
220:     if uploaded_file_size + current_chunk_size >= total_size
221:       # merge all the chunks in a background thread
222:       Jobs.enqueue_in(
223:         5.seconds,
224:         :backup_chunks_merger,
225:         filename: filename,
226:         identifier: identifier,
227:         chunks: chunk_number,
228:       )
229:     end
230: 
231:     render body: nil
232:   end
233: 
234:   def create_upload_url
235:     params.require(:filename)
236:     filename = params.fetch(:filename)
237: 
238:     unless valid_extension?(filename)
239:       return render_json_error(I18n.t("backup.backup_file_should_be_tar_gz"))
240:     end
241:     return render_json_error(I18n.t("backup.invalid_filename")) unless valid_filename?(filename)
242: 
243:     store = BackupRestore::BackupStore.create
244: 
245:     begin
246:       upload_url = store.generate_upload_url(filename)
247:     rescue BackupRestore::BackupStore::BackupFileExists
248:       return render_json_error(I18n.t("backup.file_exists"))
249:     rescue BackupRestore::BackupStore::StorageError => e
250:       return render_json_error(e)
251:     end
252: 
253:     render json: success_json.merge(url: upload_url)
254:   end
255: 
256:   private
257: 
258:   def has_enough_space_on_disk?(size)
259:     DiskSpace.free("#{Rails.root}/public/backups") > size

372:         post.uploads.each do |original_upload|
373:           optimized_1X = original_upload.optimized_images.first
374:

376:             next if !should_attach_image?(original_upload, optimized_1X)
377:             # Don't attach images that aren't rendered in the e-mail.
378:             next if is_digest && !@message.html_part.body.include?(original_upload.sha1)
379:           else
380:             # Only attach images in digests.
381:             next if is_digest
382:           end
383:

385:           next if email_size + attached_upload.filesize > max_email_size
386:

388:             path =
389:               if attached_upload.local?

391:               else
392:                 Discourse.store.download!(attached_upload)
393:               end
394: 
395:             @message_attachments_index[original_upload.sha1] = @message.attachments.size
396:             @message.attachments[original_upload.original_filename] = File.read(path)
397:             email_size += File.size(path)
398:           rescue => e
399:             Discourse.warn_exception(
400:               e,
401:               message: "Failed to attach file to email",
402:               env: {

414: 
415:     def should_attach_image?(upload, optimized_1X = nil)
416:       if !SiteSetting.secure_uploads_allow_embed_images_in_emails ||
417:            # Sometimes images in a post have a secure URL but are not secure uploads,
418:            # for example if a user uploads an image to a public post then copies the markdown
419:            # into a PM which sends an email, so we have to make sure we attached those
420:            # stripped images here as well.
421:            (
422:              !upload.secure? && !@stripped_secure_upload_shas.include?(upload.sha1) &&
423:                !@stripped_secure_upload_shas.include?(optimized_1X&.sha1)
424:            )
425:         return
426:       end
427:       if (optimized_1X&.filesize || upload.filesize) >
428:            SiteSetting.secure_uploads_max_email_embed_image_size_kb.kilobytes
429:         return
430:       end
431:       true
432:     end
433: 
434:     #
435:     # Two behaviors in the mail gem collide:
436:     #
437:     #  1. Attachments are added as extra parts at the top level,
438:     #  2. When there are both text and html parts, the content type is set
439:     #     to 'multipart/alternative'.
440:     #
441:     # Since attachments aren't alternative renderings, for emails that contain
442:     # attachments and both html and text parts, some coercing is necessary.
443:     #
444:     # When there are alternative rendering and attachments, this method causes
445:     # the top level to be 'multipart/mixed' and puts the html and text parts
446:     # into a nested 'multipart/alternative' part.
447:     #
448:     # Due to mail gem magic, @message.text_part and @message.html_part still
449:     # refer to the same objects.
450:     #
451:     # Most imporantly, we need to specify the boundary for the multipart/mixed
452:     # part of the email, otherwise we can end up with an email that appears to
453:     # be empty with the entire body attached as a single attachment, and some
454:     # mail parsers consider the entire email as a preamble/epilogue.
455:     #
456:     # c.f. https://www.w3.org/Protocols/rfc1341/7_2_Multipart.html
457:     def fix_parts_after_attachments!
458:       has_attachments = @message.attachments.present?
459:       has_alternative_renderings = @message.html_part.present? && @message.text_part.present?
460: 
461:       if has_attachments && has_alternative_renderings
462:         @message.content_type = "multipart/mixed; boundary=\"#{@message.body.boundary}\""
463: 
464:         html_part = @message.html_part
465:         @message.html_part = nil
466:         @message.parts.reject! { |p| p.content_type.start_with?("text/html") }
467: 
468:         text_part = @message.text_part
469:         @message.text_part = nil
